cards.front: What is the State Animal of West Bengal?
cards.back: Fishing Cat (Bengali: Bagh rol). Not the Royal Bengal Tiger — common exam trap!

cards.front: What is the State Bird of West Bengal?
cards.back: White-throated Kingfisher (Bengali: Maachrangi / Maachranga).

cards.front: What is the State Flower of West Bengal?
cards.back: Night Jasmine / Shiuli (Nyctanthes arbor-tristis). Blooms in autumn, used in worship.

cards.front: What is the State Tree of West Bengal?
cards.back: Chatim / Devil's Tree (Alstonia scholaris). Connected to Rabindranath Tagore and Santiniketan.

cards.front: The Damodar River is called what and why?
cards.back: 'Sorrow of Bengal' — due to severe floods it caused historically. Now controlled by DVC (Damodar Valley Corporation), modeled on TVA (USA).

cards.front: What is the Siliguri Corridor and why is it strategic?
cards.back: Called 'Chicken's Neck' — only 22-33 km wide strip connecting Northeast India to the rest of India. Sits between Bangladesh (south), Nepal (west), Bhutan (north).

cards.front: Sundarbans — what is it, where, and what UNESCO status?
cards.back: World's largest mangrove forest, shared between India (WB) and Bangladesh. UNESCO World Heritage Site. Home of Royal Bengal Tiger.

cards.front: What is the highest peak in West Bengal and its height?
cards.back: Sandakphu — 3,636 metres. Located in Darjeeling district, on the Nepal border.

cards.front: The Teesta River is significant for what dispute?
cards.back: Water-sharing dispute between India and Bangladesh. Teesta flows through Sikkim and North Bengal before entering Bangladesh. Agreement still pending.

cards.front: Farakka Barrage — what is it and on which river?
cards.back: Built on the Ganges at Farakka, Murshidabad. Controls water flow to Hooghly river (India) vs Padma (Bangladesh). Causes water-sharing dispute.

cards.front: How many districts does West Bengal have?
cards.back: 23 districts (after recent reorganization). Kolkata is the smallest by area, South 24 Parganas is the largest.

cards.front: West Bengal shares its longest international border with which country?
cards.back: Bangladesh — approximately 2,509 km. WB is the only Indian state that borders 3 countries: Bangladesh, Nepal (tiny section), and Bhutan.

cards.front: Haldia Port is in which district and why is it significant?
cards.back: Purba Medinipur. It is India's largest riverine port and a major petrochemical hub. Handles cargo that reduces load from Kolkata Port.

cards.front: Digha is located in which district and is famous for?
cards.back: Purba Medinipur. It is the nearest beach resort to Kolkata, on the Bay of Bengal coast.

cards.front: Santiniketan is in which district, founded by whom?
cards.back: Birbhum district. Founded by Rabindranath Tagore. Visva-Bharati University is located here. UNESCO World Heritage Site (2023).

cards.front: Bishnupur is famous for what and located in which district?
cards.back: Bankura district. Famous for terracotta temples (Dakshina-Dihi, Rasmancha) and Baluchari sarees. Also known for Bankura horse (terracotta toy).

cards.front: Hazarduari Palace is in which district and who built it?
cards.back: Murshidabad district. Built by Nawab Nazim Humayun Jah (1829-37). Has 1000 doors (hazarduari means 1000 doors), 900 of which are false.

cards.front: The Rarh region of Bengal includes which districts?
cards.back: Bankura, Purulia, Birbhum, and parts of Burdwan. It's the ancient hard-rock plateau area, less fertile than the delta, known for tribal culture.

cards.front: West Bengal's state song — what is it and who composed it?
cards.back: 'Amar Shonar Bangla' — composed by Rabindranath Tagore. Also happens to be Bangladesh's national anthem.

cards.front: Dooars region is in which part of WB and known for?
cards.back: North Bengal (Jalpaiguri, Alipurduar districts). Known for tea gardens, elephant corridors, and forest reserves like Gorumara and Buxa.

cards.front: Which river separates West Bengal from Bangladesh near Kolkata?
cards.back: Ichamati River marks part of the WB-Bangladesh border near North 24 Parganas.

cards.front: Kangsabati (Kasai) river flows through which district and has what project?
cards.back: Purba and Paschim Medinipur. Kangsabati reservoir project (Mukutmanipur dam) — one of WB's major irrigation projects.

cards.front: What is the Terai region in North Bengal?
cards.back: Narrow belt between Darjeeling Himalayan foothills and the plains. Rich in forests and biodiversity. Districts: Darjeeling, Jalpaiguri foothills.

cards.front: Which district has the highest population density in West Bengal?
cards.back: North 24 Parganas (adjacent to Kolkata). It is also one of the most urbanized districts in India.

cards.front: WB's Mahananda River — where does it flow and where does it end?
cards.back: Flows through Darjeeling and Malda districts. It is a tributary of the Ganges and marks the border between Malda (WB) and Bihar near Farakka.
